channel.title: 简报 Jae Daily · 2026年06月22日
channel.description: 国际政治 · 宏观财经 · 前沿科技 · AI 辅助分析

item.title: [未译] Five ways the Iran peace deal could affect you and your money
item.description: With fuel and gas prices having fallen in recent days, we look at how the end of hostilities might affect you - in five charts.

item.title: [未译] Colombia's escalating, brutal internal conflict is defining its presidential election
item.description: A left-wing senator who backs talks with armed gangs faces an outsider endorsed by Trump.

item.title: [未译] Half of France under red heat alert as alcohol banned at street music festival
item.description: Temperatures are forecast to peak on Monday, and authorities warn they could match historic highs.

item.title: [未译] Brain-computer interface trials are taking off
item.description: This week, I covered the story of Casey Harrell—a man with ALS who is “the first power user” of a brain implant, according to the researchers who worked with him. Harrell is paralyzed and unable to sp

item.title: [未译] Former Olympian denies vandalising Washington Reflecting Pool after arrest
item.description: Davey Hearn says he was simply touching the new paint at the site out of curiosity and did not remove or alter it.

item.title: [未译] Singapore Dollar Set to Gain Despite Hawkish Fed, Analysts Say
item.description: The Singapore dollar is poised to strengthen against the US dollar in the second half of the year despite a hawkish Federal Reserve boosting sentiment toward the greenback, according to strategists.

item.title: [未译] Ubisoft co-founder Claude Guillemot dies in plane crash
item.description: Claude Guillemot, who founded Ubisoft with his four brothers, has died at the age of 69.

item.title: [未译] Is anyone still using Emacs?
item.description: <p><a href="https://lobste.rs/s/s1ep1w/is_anyone_still_using_emacs">Comments</a></p>

item.title: [未译] The creative director of Chanel shuns the ‘Wild West’ look: ‘We already have too many cowboys in this world’
item.description: EL PAÍS went inside Thomas du Pré de Saint Maur’s headquarters. He is the creative mind behind Chanel’s No. 5 fragrance, which remains timeless. During this interview, he describes how he recruited su

item.title: [未译] Is Germany looking again at coal-powered electricity?
item.description: It had planned to abandon the fuel, but the higher cost of natural gas may make it think again.

item.title: [未译] Eileen Gu on Values That Define Success
item.description: Skiing synthetizes all of the values and reflects back to me those values I judge myself by,” Olympic gold medalist Eileen Gu tells Bloomberg in an exclusive interview in Hong Kong. The freestyle skie

item.title: [未译] 【今朝の5本】仕事を始める前に読んでおきたい厳選ニュース
item.description: 米イラン和平協議、ホルムズ原油輸送続く、英首相が近く退任表明か、重要鉱物、日産取締役案に不支持

item.title: [未译] Renault Pushback on Nissan Board Picks Targets Creditor Mizuho
item.description: Renault SA’s push against two of Nissan Motor Co.’s appointments to the board effectively pits the Japanese carmaker’s top shareholder against its biggest creditor.

item.title: [未译] Germany, KNDS Family Owners Agree on Stake Sale Ahead of IPO
item.description: Families that own half of tankmaker KNDS NV agreed to sell a 40% stake to the German government, according to people familiar with the matter, paving the way for one of Europe’s key defense companies

item.title: [未译] Fuel sales halted in occupied Crimea as Ukraine targets oil facilities
item.description: Fuel had already been rationed due to shortages caused by Kyiv's attacks against supply routes in Russian-occupied territories.

item.title: [未译] Pixar's 'Toy Story 5' lassos biggest opening weekend in franchise history with $160 million haul
item.description: Disney and Pixar's "Toy Story 5" tallied $160 million domestically during its opening weekend, the highest debut in franchise history.

item.title: [未译] Burnham’s ‘King of the North’ tactics will be clipped if he becomes U.K. prime minister
item.description: After winning a seat in parliament for his local area of Makerfield, Burnham, hopes to ride a level of popularity rarely enjoyed by politicians to become Britain's next leader.

item.title: [未译] Ethiopia PM's party wins landslide as fears grow of new conflict
item.description: Abiy Ahmed's party retains its huge majority despite unrest in several parts of Ethiopia and tensions with its neighbours.

item.title: [未译] Israeli strikes kill six people in Gaza including Al Jazeera cameraman, officials say
item.description: The Israeli military accused Ahmed Wishah of being a "Hamas sniper operative", without providing evidence.

item.title: [未译] Polymarket reportedly paid people to post fake videos of themselves placing bets
item.description: According to a Wall Street Journal investigation, Polymarket has been paying people to film themselves placing fake bets and celebrating fake wins on social media. WSJ id

item.title: [未译] Tight security as Indian students resit medical exam after alleged paper leak
item.description: Millions of candidates face biometric checks and frisking as air force deployed to secure exam papers.

item.title: [未译] Japan’s AI goldrush faces backlash as data centers sprout up in urban areas
item.description: Residents living next to new developments are raising concerns over environmental and health issues linked to the "factories" of today.

item.title: [未译] O'Leary extends Ryanair contract in deal that could net him over £130m
item.description: The Ryanair boss extends his contract to 2032, in a deal featuring a bonus scheme that could earn him more than €150m (£130m).

item.title: [未译] Yamal scores first World Cup goal as Spain bounce back with thumping win over Saudi Arabia
item.description: The European champions put their shock opening draw against Cape Verde behind them with a crushing 4-0 defeat of Saudi Arabia on Sunday to move top of Group H and kick-start their World Cup campaign.

item.title: [未译] Plans to end gazumping with binding agreements in house sales shake-up
item.description: Sales agreements will be legally binding sooner and making sellers provide more home information up front are part of the planned changes.

item.title: [未译] A startup claims it broke through a bottleneck that’s holding back LLMs
item.description: Miami-based AI startup Subquadratic came out of stealth mode last month with a huge claim. It announced that it had solved a mathematical bottleneck that had been holding back large language models fo

item.title: [未译] The UK will scan asylum-seekers’ faces for age checks—despite knowing the tech is flawed
item.description: Tests of age-verification technology show the risks of life-altering errors.

item.title: [未译] Warning over 'fragile' public finances as borrowing rises
item.description: The UK borrowed £23.3bn in May, according to official figures, up almost a third on the same month last year.

item.title: [未译] 'I'd be put off if he asked to split it': Who should pay on a first date?
item.description: Some insist on splitting the bill, others say the asker should pay, while many still see a man paying as romantic.

item.title: [未译] Hong Kong to roll out measures boosting offshore yuan trading in July: finance chief
item.description: As Hong Kong marks the 29th anniversary of its return to Chinese rule on July 1, the South China Morning Post talks to the city’s senior officials about the administration’s achievements so far and wh
